source_jobs.title: Computer Vision Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CIVILGRID

CivilGrid is the trusted civil design platform powering affordable infrastructure renewal and growth. We give utilities, civil engineers, and municipalities a single, collaborative source of truth for what's underground so teams can plan, design, and build faster, smarter, and safer.



Our platform consolidates utility records, geotechnical and environmental data, paving moratoriums, and jurisdictional requirements directly from 3,000+ utilities, agencies, and municipalities with full chain-of-custody documentation. Teams at PG&E, Mark Thomas, and dozens of leading engineering firms use CivilGrid to cut research time by up to 88%, reduce project costs, prevent dig-ins, and accelerate delivery on the projects that keep communities running.



We're a venture-backed Series A startup tackling a $4.7T US infrastructure market that has been underserved by software for decades. Founded by industry insiders, each with 10+ years in utilities and civil engineering, we move fast, ship work that matters, and stay close to the customers and field teams we build for.



WHY THIS ROLE



As a small team, we all wear many hats and enjoy tackling new challenges. When it comes to tech, we're seeking experienced individuals who can efficiently identify problems and devise solutions. As a Computer Vision Engineer, you will get the chance to work on challenging problems that directly contribute to the scalability of CivilGrid's products, and be one of the first engineers shaping CivilGrid's computer vision capabilities.



This role reports to the Head of Product & Engineering. It is a hybrid role with expectations that you are working from our Boston, MA office at least 2 days per week if hired.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Explore our data and workflows to identify opportunities where CV/ML-based solutions can have the most impact

 - Apply classical computer vision techniques — feature matching, geometric transformations, image registration — to build internal and customer-facing features

 - Prepare training data, and train, fine-tune, and validate vision-based models

 - Develop cross-technology solutions that combine VLMs, vision-based models, and classical CV to solve problems no single approach can

 - Ship CV pipelines that can be continuously monitored and validated, with evaluation harnesses and quality metrics built in from day one



WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - 3 years of experience building computer vision systems in Python, with tools such as OpenCV, NumPy, and PyTorch (or equivalents)

 - Solid grounding in classical computer vision: feature extraction and matching, geometric transformations, image registration, and robust estimation (e.g., RANSAC)

 - Solid grounding in ML and ML-based computer vision: CNNs, transformers, embedding spaces

 - Hands-on experience preparing datasets and training, fine-tuning, and evaluating vision-based models

 - Start Up Ready: autonomous, moves fast, takes initiative without being asked

 - Strong verbal and written communication, able to articulate ideas clearly and collaborate effectively

 - A degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or a related field (B.S. or M.S.)

BONUS POINTS

 - Experience working with messy real-world imagery — scanned documents, OCR outputs, low-quality rasters — is a huge plus

 - Familiarity with geospatial tooling (GDAL/rasterio, coordinate reference systems)

 - Experience integrating VLM/LLM APIs into production workflows

 - Experience with MLOps



HOW WE WORK

We're a small, but rapidly growing team that ships. Expect direct feedback, real ownership from day one, and proximity to customers — huge utilities, public agencies, and engineering firms whose work depends on what we build. We default to in-person collaboration where it matters and remote-friendly flexibility otherwise. We hire people who care deeply about their craft and the communities our customers serve.



COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

The base salary range for this role is $150,000– $2

source_jobs.title: Senior Account Executive - Utilities
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CIVILGRID

CivilGrid is the trusted civil design platform powering affordable infrastructure renewal and growth. We give utilities, civil engineers, and municipalities a single, collaborative source of truth for what's underground so teams can plan, design, and build faster, smarter, and safer.



Our platform consolidates utility records, geotechnical and environmental data, paving moratoriums, and jurisdictional requirements directly from 3,000+ utilities, agencies, and municipalities with full chain-of-custody documentation. Teams at PG&E, Mark Thomas, and dozens of leading engineering firms use CivilGrid to cut research time by up to 88%, reduce project costs, prevent dig-ins, and accelerate delivery on the projects that keep communities running.



We’re a venture-backed Series A startup tackling a $4.7T US infrastructure market that has been underserved by software for decades. Founded by industry insiders, each with 10+ years in utilities and civil engineering, we move fast, ship work that matters, and stay close to the customers and field teams we build for.



WHY THIS ROLE

We’re seeking a highly motivated and outcomes-driven Senior Account Executive to own and grow CivilGrid’s presence across the US West utilities market. The ideal candidate has a proven track record of enterprise sales success within the utility or energy sector, thrives in complex multi-threaded deals, and brings creativity and persistence to every stage of the sales process.



This role is purpose-built for a seller who understands how utilities operate — from capital planning cycles and GIS/records teams to procurement, compliance, and engineering leadership. You’ll be selling into some of the largest infrastructure owners in the country, helping them prevent dig-ins, reduce project costs, and accelerate capital delivery with CivilGrid’s civil design platform.



This role reports to our Head of Sales and is specifically focused on investor-owned and large public utilities (electric, gas, and water) in the US West region (WA, OR, CA, AZ, ID, NV, UT). Although this is a remote position, you must be based in the US West to be considered.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Deliver and exceed sales targets with a strong, quantifiable history of success

Lead complex enterprise sales cycles from prospecting through contract execution, including:

 - Securing executive sponsorship across engineering, capital planning, and GIS leadership

 - Running pilots with clear success criteria tied to utility-specific KPIs (dig-in reduction, research time, map request throughput)

 - Converting pilots into multi-year enterprise contracts

 - Develop creative, targeted outbound campaigns tailored to utility buyer personas — VP Engineering, Capital Portfolio Managers, GIS Directors, and Records Teams

 - Navigate utility procurement processes, including security reviews, IT compliance, and vendor onboarding requirements common to regulated utilities

 - Present compelling sales materials including executive presentations, ROI analyses, and business cases demonstrating measurable impact on capital project delivery and dig-in prevention

 - Leverage MEDDPIC to develop opportunity and account-level strategies and maintain up-to-date account plans.

 - Maintain stakeholder engagement throughout long and complex utility sales cycles, with patience, persistence, and strategies to accelerate timelines around capital planning windows and paving moratorium cycles

 - Represent CivilGrid at utility and energy industry conferences with a strategic, outcome-focused approach to networking and prospecting

 - Leverage and expand an existing personal network in the utility/energy space to open new opportunities and drive pipeline growth



WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 5-10 years of enterprise sales experience with a strong record of hitting or exceeding quota

 - Demonstrated experience selling software or technology solutions directly into electric, gas, or water utilities — this is a hard

source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CIVILGRID

CivilGrid is the trusted civil design platform powering affordable infrastructure renewal and growth. We give utilities, civil engineers, and municipalities a single, collaborative source of truth for what's underground so teams can plan, design, and build faster, smarter, and safer.



Our platform consolidates utility records, geotechnical and environmental data, paving moratoriums, and jurisdictional requirements directly from 3,000+ utilities, agencies, and municipalities with full chain-of-custody documentation. Teams at PG&E, Mark Thomas, and dozens of leading engineering firms use CivilGrid to cut research time by up to 88%, reduce project costs, prevent dig-ins, and accelerate delivery on the projects that keep communities running.



We're a venture-backed Series A startup tackling a $4.7T US infrastructure market that has been underserved by software for decades. Founded by industry insiders, each with 10+ years in utilities and civil engineering, we move fast, ship work that matters, and stay close to the customers and field teams we build for.



WHY THIS ROLE

CivilGrid is at an inflection point. We’ve proven product-market fit with major utilities and leading engineering firms — and now we’re building the marketing foundation to match the pace of our growth. This is the first dedicated product marketing hire, and the person who fills it will shape how the market understands and buys CivilGrid. You’ll own the positioning, messaging, and launch motions for a platform that genuinely changes how critical infrastructure gets built. The upstream impact is real: the projects CivilGrid accelerates are the roads, pipelines, and utilities that communities depend on.



This role reports to the Head of Marketing. It is a remote position in the US with a strong preference for candidates in the San Francisco Bay Area.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own positioning and messaging end-to-end — develop and maintain the core narrative for CivilGrid’s platform and products, keeping it sharp, differentiated, and grounded in what buyers actually care about across utilities, AEC firms, and municipalities.

 - Drive product launch motions — lead go-to-market planning and execution for new features and products, from launch brief through sales enablement and campaign materials, in tight collaboration with Product and Sales.

 - Build and maintain the sales enablement library — create and continuously improve the decks, battlecards, one-pagers, objection-handling guides, and competitive intelligence that help the revenue team win deals and shorten cycles.

 - Translate customer and market insight into strategy — run win/loss interviews, buyer research, and ICP refinement to ensure messaging reflects how customers actually describe their problems and evaluate solutions.

 - Develop product content that earns credibility — produce case studies, solution briefs, demo scripts, and technical explainers that speak the language of civil engineers, utility planners, and project managers.

 - Support campaign strategy for launch and growth motions — partner with the content marketer to develop campaign concepts, messaging frameworks, and audience segmentation; you’re not the campaign manager, but you set the strategic foundation.

 - Grow the function as CivilGrid scales — help define what best-in-class product marketing looks like here, build the playbooks, and eventually help grow the team as headcount expands.



WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - 4-6 years of product marketing experience in B2B industrial or construction technology — SaaS experience strongly preferred.

 - Hands-on experience marketing preconstruction workflows, products, or tooling — either as a practitioner who has run these processes or as a marketer who has sold into this buying context. This is the most important requirement.

 - Demonstrated domain fluency in at least one of our core buyer verticals: electric, gas, or water utilities; civil/transportation engineering firms;

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CIVILGRID

CivilGrid is the trusted civil design platform powering affordable infrastructure renewal and growth. We give utilities, civil engineers, and municipalities a single, collaborative source of truth for what's underground so teams can plan, design, and build faster, smarter, and safer.



Our platform consolidates utility records, geotechnical and environmental data, paving moratoriums, and jurisdictional requirements directly from 3,000+ utilities, agencies, and municipalities with full chain-of-custody documentation. Teams at PG&E, Mark Thomas, and dozens of leading engineering firms use CivilGrid to cut research time by up to 88%, reduce project costs, prevent dig-ins, and accelerate delivery on the projects that keep communities running.



We're a venture-backed Series A startup tackling a $4.7T US infrastructure market that has been underserved by software for decades. Founded by industry insiders, each with 10+ years in utilities and civil engineering, we move fast, ship work that matters, and stay close to the customers and field teams we build for.



WHY THIS ROLE

As a small team, we all wear many hats and enjoy tackling new challenges. When it comes to tech, we're seeking experienced individuals who can efficiently identify problems and devise solutions. As a Full Stack Engineer, you will be a foundational team member with the opportunity to work on all aspects of the application and shape the technical future of CivilGrid. Joining at this stage, you will build core systems that will help us scale quickly, and benefit from tremendous upside as we grow.



This role reports to the Head of Product & Engineering. It is a hybrid role with expectations that you are working from our Boston, MA office at least 2 days per week if hired.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and build end-to-end features, covering everything from database models to user-facing components using React

 - Tackle a variety of areas: web and mobile applications, mapping interfaces, data processing pipelines, algorithmic relevancy, and real-time collaboration tools

 - Profile and tune performance across the entire stack to ensure efficient and responsive systems

 - Implement and maintain analytics, health monitoring, alerts, and logging to maintain operational visibility and system reliability



WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - 3 years of experience working with JavaScript or TypeScript, particularly with React and Node.js (or equivalent frameworks)

 - Start Up Ready: Autonomous, moves fast, takes initiative without being asked

 - Experience working on a complex B2B Product

 - Strong foundational knowledge of Postgres is a huge plus

 - Familiarity with geospatial frameworks (PostGIS, GeoJSON, map rendering libraries, etc.) is also a plus

 - Conduct code reviews, mentor junior engineers, and offer constructive feedback to help grow the team’s technical capabilities

 - Strong verbal and written communication, able to articulate ideas clearly and collaborate effectively

 - A degree in Computer Science, Civil Engineering, or a related field (B.S. or M.S.)



HOW WE WORK

We're a small, but rapidly growing team that ships. Expect direct feedback, real ownership from day one, and proximity to customers — huge utilities, public agencies, and engineering firms whose work depends on what we build. We default to in-person collaboration where it matters and remote-friendly flexibility otherwise. We hire people who care deeply about their craft and the communities our customers serve.



COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

The base salary range for this role is $137,700 - $208,200.



Base salary is only one part of your total compensation package at CivilGrid, which also includes meaningful early-stage equity, comprehensive benefits, and the opportunity to shape a category-defining company. The base salary range listed above may encompass various levels and/or geographic locations; final offers are determined based on the candidate's experience, skills, level, a

source_jobs.title: Digitization Project Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CIVILGRID

CivilGrid is the trusted civil design platform powering affordable infrastructure renewal and growth. We give utilities, civil engineers, and municipalities a single, collaborative source of truth for what's underground so teams can plan, design, and build faster, smarter, and safer.



Our platform consolidates utility records, geotechnical and environmental data, paving moratoriums, and jurisdictional requirements directly from 3,000+ utilities, agencies, and municipalities with full chain-of-custody documentation. Teams at PG&E, Mark Thomas, and dozens of leading engineering firms use CivilGrid to cut research time by up to 88%, reduce project costs, prevent dig-ins, and accelerate delivery on the projects that keep communities running.



We're a venture-backed Series A startup tackling a $4.7T US infrastructure market that has been underserved by software for decades. Founded by industry insiders, each with 10+ years in utilities and civil engineering, we move fast, ship work that matters, and stay close to the customers and field teams we build for.



WHY THIS ROLE

As a Digitization Project Manager, you will bridge the gap between historical utility data and actionable digital insights. By translating complex subsurface utility records into GIS and CAD formats, you will directly empower our customers to build safer and faster. Additionally, you will take on key program and project management responsibilities, stepping up to mentor junior employees to foster a culture of technical and operational excellence.



This is a customer and delivery-focused role. You’ll own a portfolio of customers and projects and make sure CivilGrid’s insights are delivered to them on-time and with high quality. This role reports to the Head of Product Operations. It is a remote position in the US or Canada with a strong preference for candidates in Kansas City, Charlotte, or Denver.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

You will be a key player on a specialized team of industry practitioners, bringing deep Subsurface Utility Engineering (SUE) expertise and operating in our fast-moving startup environment.



Your core role is Project & Delivery Management:

 - Provide expert-level markup, interpretation, and guidance of complex utility engineering records, as-builts, and historical plan sets.

 - Conduct rigorous QA/QC on digitized deliverables to ensure absolute accuracy before they reach the CivilGrid platform.

 - Manage and lead delivery-focused teams (Customer Success, GIS, Utilities Research, etc.) to complete utilities research projects. You own the scheduling, prioritization, and resource allocation for your projects.

 - Mentor junior employees by sharing your deep industry and technical knowledge, and coach them through completing complex projects.

 - Design and implement QAQC frameworks and data validation pipelines.



WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - Civil and Utilities Expertise: 5+ years of experience in civil design, subsurface utilities engineering, and utilities mapping.

 - The Process Builder Mindset: You love taking complex, unstructured problems and creating order out of them. You’re not afraid to tackle uncertainty, but your instinct is to organize and systematize.

 - Broad Technical Proficiency: You have a working understanding and user competency in GIS, CAD, and other industry data and design tools. You’re proficient with project management software (we use Linear).

 - Humble and Collaborative: You have a "sweep the floors" mentality. No task is beneath you if it helps the team deliver for the customer. You communicate complex technical blockers plainly and constructively.



HOW WE WORK

We're a small, but rapidly growing team that ships. Expect direct feedback, real ownership from day one, and proximity to customers — huge utilities, public agencies, and engineering firms whose work depends on what we build. We default to in-person collaboration where it matters and remote-friendly flexibility otherwise. We hire people who

source_jobs.title: Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CIVILGRID

CivilGrid is the trusted civil design platform powering affordable infrastructure renewal and growth. We give utilities, civil engineers, and municipalities a single, collaborative source of truth for what's underground so teams can plan, design, and build faster, smarter, and safer.



Our platform consolidates utility records, geotechnical and environmental data, paving moratoriums, and jurisdictional requirements directly from 3,000+ utilities, agencies, and municipalities with full chain-of-custody documentation. Teams at PG&E, Mark Thomas, and dozens of leading engineering firms use CivilGrid to cut research time by up to 88%, reduce project costs, prevent dig-ins, and accelerate delivery on the projects that keep communities running.



We're a venture-backed Series A startup tackling a $4.7T US infrastructure market that has been underserved by software for decades. Founded by industry insiders, each with 10+ years in utilities and civil engineering, we move fast, ship work that matters, and stay close to the customers and field teams we build for.



WHY THIS ROLE

As a small team, we all wear many hats and enjoy tackling new challenges. We're seeking an experienced Engineering Manager who thrives in a hands-on environment who’ll shape the direction of the engineering organization and how we operate as we scale. We don't expect you to have a background in everything we use, but we do expect strong technical skills, exceptional communication, and a track record of leading high-performing engineering teams through ambiguity and scale. We're looking for builders who still know how to code even if it's not part of the day-to-day.



This role reports to the Head of Product & Engineering. It is a hybrid role with expectations that you are working from our Boston, MA office at least 2 days per week if hired.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Leading and developing a fast-paced engineering team, providing clear direction, feedback, mentorship, and coaching

 - Owning planning and execution across projects while helping engineers stay unblocked, focused, and aligned

 - Defining and evolving our hiring process, standards, and team structure as we grow

 - Building the processes, expectations, and culture that will support CivilGrid's next phase of growth

 - Our tech stack:
   
   - React, React Native, Tailwind
   
   - Bun.sh http://Bun.sh servers deployed on AWS ECS, Postgres on RDS
   
   - ESRI and Mapbox for mapping
   
   - Proprietary mapping data service built for speed and customizability
   
   - GitHub, Slack, Notion, Figma



WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - 6+ years of experience in software engineering

 - You’re hands on and have a minimum of 3 years experience in engineering management

 - Proven track record of growing and managing high-performing engineering teams

 - Comfortable in a greenfield environment and excited to shape, design, and stand up new systems

 - Strong communication skills - verbal and written

 - Have a few war stories to share on running high scale production systems

 - BS/MS in Computer Science or similar

BONUS POINTS

 - Strong preference for candidates who have managed senior and staff level engineers

 - Prior startup or founder experience



HOW WE WORK

We're a small, but rapidly growing team that ships. Expect direct feedback, real ownership from day one, and proximity to customers — huge utilities, public agencies, and engineering firms whose work depends on what we build. We default to in-person collaboration where it matters and remote-friendly flexibility otherwise. We hire people who care deeply about their craft and the communities our customers serve.



COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

The base salary range for this role is $173,806 - $236,250.



Base salary is only one part of your total compensation package at CivilGrid, which also includes meaningful early-stage equity, comprehensive benefits, and the opportunity to shape a category-defining company. The base salary range listed

notes: CivilGrid is a civil design platform with a strong engineering core, posting a mix of technical and non-technical roles.
